roadmapScope: Planning only. No L5 proof implementation is introduced by this artefact.

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: Postgres restart preserves committed tenant rows and replayed L3 behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: Postgres query timeout returns typed failure without partial state mutation
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: Postgres transient connection loss retries idempotent operations without duplicate rows
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: Postgres concurrent mutations preserve RLS/tenant isolation and state diff invariants
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: Postgres unavailable mode reports degraded readiness and blocks unsafe writes
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: Postgres reconnect restores certified L4 behaviour using existing migrations/schema
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: Postgres backup/restore preserves tenant state, audit rows, and L4 replay contract
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: Postgres failover or reconnection returns to certified L4 baseline

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: Redis restart preserves or explicitly rebuilds rate-limit/session-derived state
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: Redis timeout degrades safely without granting extra quota or access
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: Redis transient failure retries without double-counting counters
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: Redis concurrent increments preserve atomic limit semantics
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: Redis unavailable mode fails closed for rate limiting and exposes degraded readiness
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: Redis recovery resumes counters consistently with documented TTL semantics
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: Redis volatile state non-applicability is documented or snapshot restore is asserted where configured
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: Redis failover preserves atomic counter semantics after reconnect

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: MinIO restart preserves tenant object prefixes and signed-object behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: MinIO timeout returns typed storage failure without exposing partial downloads
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: MinIO retry completes idempotent object writes without duplicate tenant-visible objects
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: MinIO concurrent tenant object operations preserve prefix isolation
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: MinIO unavailable mode blocks unsafe object mutations and reports degraded readiness
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: MinIO recovery restores object read/write/delete lifecycle behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: MinIO backup/restore preserves object bytes, metadata, and tenant prefix boundaries
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: MinIO failover/reconnect preserves object lifecycle semantics

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: OpenBao restart preserves secret readability only after valid unseal/health state
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: OpenBao timeout fails closed for secret reads and writes
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: OpenBao transient failure retries do not duplicate secret versions unexpectedly
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: OpenBao concurrent secret rotations preserve version and permission semantics
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: OpenBao unavailable mode blocks secret-dependent mutations and reports degraded readiness
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: OpenBao recovery restores typed secret resolution and audit behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: OpenBao backup/restore preserves secret metadata and access boundaries
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: OpenBao failover preserves secret access policy and sealed/unsealed safety

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: Keycloak restart preserves realm discovery, issuer, JWKS, and client configuration behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: Keycloak timeout triggers static fallback only where explicitly allowed and fails closed otherwise
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: Keycloak transient authz failure retries without granting unauthorised access
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: Keycloak concurrent auth checks preserve tenant and policy boundaries
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: Keycloak unavailable mode distinguishes degraded fallback from sole-UMA fail-closed routes
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: Keycloak recovery restores OIDC discovery and policy-decision behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: Keycloak realm/client backup restore preserves issuer and callback semantics
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: Keycloak failover preserves realm discovery and policy decision behaviour

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: Temporal restart preserves workflow state and resumes scheduled work
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: Temporal activity timeout records typed failure and respects retry policy
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: Temporal retry resumes workflow without duplicate side effects
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: Temporal concurrent workflow starts preserve workflow ID idempotency
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: Temporal unavailable mode reports degraded workflow readiness and queues or blocks work safely
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: Temporal recovery resumes workflow execution from durable history
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: Temporal persistence backup/restore preserves workflow histories where configured
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: Temporal worker or service failover resumes durable workflow progress

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: Windmill restart preserves job visibility and access-boundary behaviour
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: Windmill job timeout records failure without orphaned privileged execution
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: Windmill retry preserves idempotent automation semantics
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: Windmill concurrent job submissions preserve tenant and permission boundaries
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: Windmill unavailable mode blocks automation mutations and reports degraded readiness
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: Windmill recovery resumes safe job dispatch and preserves audit trail
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: Windmill backup/restore preserves job definitions and access controls where configured
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: Windmill worker failover does not duplicate non-idempotent jobs

substrateScenarioCatalogue.restartScenario: Observability stack restart preserves scrape/readiness and log/trace ingestion continuity
substrateScenarioCatalogue.timeoutScenario: Observability timeout does not block core mutations and records degraded telemetry state
substrateScenarioCatalogue.retryScenario: Observability export retries avoid duplicate misleading metric samples where possible
substrateScenarioCatalogue.concurrencyScenario: Observability concurrent telemetry ingestion preserves tenant correlation labels
substrateScenarioCatalogue.degradedOperationScenario: Observability unavailable mode reports degraded visibility without fabricating green health
substrateScenarioCatalogue.recoveryScenario: Observability recovery resumes audit, metric, trace, and log correlation visibility
substrateScenarioCatalogue.backupRestoreScenario: Observability retention/backup preserves incident-relevant telemetry where configured
substrateScenarioCatalogue.failoverScenario: Observability failover preserves alerting and trace/log query continuity where applicable

recommendedFirstTarget.rationale: Highest-risk, lowest-order substrate target for beginning resilience proof work.
